temas.nota: The theme is saved on this device.
idioma.nota: When applied, the whole interface will switch to the selected language.
modo.descripcion:
Pure Structural (EP): automatic sign (±) and no magnitude clipping.
Reference-Normalized (NR): clamps the ratio to [0,1] and applies ±0.999…990; if there are overflows it suggests redefining the minimum/maximum.
resultados.nota: In the UC Scale, 0 is an unreachable asymptotic limit; we always work with minimal/maximal expressions ±0.000…010 and ±0.999…990.
si.modal.p1: What are the Sᵢ? The Sᵢ model proposes 16 simultaneous structural functions (S₁…S₁₆) that act like a “grammar” to describe coherence dynamics: from base vibration and initial aggregation to maximum projection. The proposal aims for a common language to study physical, biological, cognitive, emotional, or symbolic processes through a structural reading.
si.modal.p2: The architecture is organized around a core (S₉) that works as a threshold/pulse: the cycle starts at S₉ and returns to S₉. A central trigger activates extremes (S₁ and S₁₆) and a simultaneous return occurs along two parallel trajectories, represented here as Chain A and Chain B.
si.modal.p3: Each Sᵢ can be associated with a mathematical family (parameterized by r) to simulate or visualize a “profile” as bars or ellipses. In this app you can enter values manually, generate values algorithmically, save patterns, apply templates, and enable auto-derivations to close the cycle upon visualization (for example, for S₁ and S₁₆).
si.modal.b1: The default names of Sᵢ are functional labels: you can assign your own nomenclature (depending on the system you study) so the model is “readable” in your domain.
si.modal.b2: Use Templates to load thematic nomenclatures and Patterns to save/restore value sets (useful to compare states or iterations).
si.modal.b3: Interpret the set as a structural “signature”: by comparing Chain A vs B and observing bars/ellipses, you can detect symmetries, asymmetries, thresholds, and phase changes.

viz.nota: OFF ⇒ “pure r” (no x). Use ↑ ↓ ← → to rotate the 3D viewer.
viz.resetTitle: Reset pan/zoom; Shift+click: also resets 3D angles
rt.nota: It reflects in Sᵢ and in both viewers. You can revert to the previous value.
cu.p1: What is the UC Scale? The Universal Coherence (UC) Scale is a proposed structural metric with limited precision to express, in a common format, the coherence of heterogeneous phenomena (physical, biological, cognitive, emotional, or symbolic). Its focus is not raw “magnitude”, but the degree of relational alignment (how much internal noise/friction remains and how much pattern/order emerges) within a defined frame.
cu.p2: In UC, a value is always interpreted within a reference frame: you define observable Min and Max (in the same context), and the Observed value is placed relative to that interval. This is intentional: the coherence shown is contextual. If you change the frame (conditions, time window, Min/Max criterion), the UC value changes.
cu.p3: Structural center (Cₛ): 0.000000000000000 works as an ideal asymptotic limit (unreachable). To work stably in a digital environment, a finite operational range is used to avoid singularities: ±0.000000000000010 ↔ ±0.999999999999990 (16 digits).
cu.p4: Coherent scaling law: the UC value is obtained by normalizing the position of Observed between Min and Max via the ratio (Obs−Min)/(Max−Min) and projecting it to the operational range with the factor ±0.999…990. The sign (±) is not decorative: it is used to indicate membership/inversion (for example, when Observed lies outside [Min, Max] or when the range is inverted).
cu.p5: Limited precision and layered reading: UC uses a fixed notation of 16 digits (not counting sign or dot) and often reserves the last decimal as “0” to stabilize rounding. It can also be read “in layers”: each decimal is understood as a layer or potentially nestable level (fractal reading), where part of the number can act as a sub-scale inside another.
cu.p6: EP and NR modes (in this app): Pure Structural (EP) does not clip the ratio: it shows the structure even with overflows. Reference-Normalized (NR) clamps the ratio to [0,1] before projecting, keeping the result within the operational range. If warnings appear for being out of range, it usually means you should redefine Min/Max (or swap values) so the frame represents what you want to compare.
calc.titulo: UC Calculator (step by step) — extended guide
calc.s1: 1) Define the frame: decide what you are comparing and under what conditions. The trio Observed/Min/Max must belong to the same frame (same source, same unit, same time window or Min/Max criterion).
calc.s2: 2) UC format (16 digits): write each value with exactly 16 digits (sign and dot not counted). The app helps with the /16 counter and examples like 0.000000000000010 and 0.999999999999990.
calc.s3: 3) Validate the range: make sure Max > Min. If Observed falls below Min or exceeds Max, the app will show warnings to help you reinterpret the data (e.g., use Observed as the new minimum or maximum).
calc.s4: 4) Choose a mode: EP keeps the ratio as-is (no clipping). NR clamps the ratio to [0,1] before projecting, to avoid overflows and keep the result within the operational range.
calc.s5: 5) Compute the relative position: Δ = Obs − Min, R = Max − Min, q = Δ/R. That q expresses the position of Observed between the extremes.
calc.s6: 6) UC projection: the app projects q to the operational range by multiplying by the factor ±0.999999999999990. The sign is assigned automatically based on membership/inversion of the range.
calc.s7: 7) Read UC and UC ext.: UC value is shown in the operational format (16 digits). UC ext. expands decimals for fine inspection, keeping the same structural logic.
calc.note: Tip: use the Level and Dec indicators as a quick reading of “layer/order” and of the first significant decimal. If you work by layers, part of the decimals can be reused as a nested sub-scale in later analyses.
calc.doi: Essay (foundation of UC scaling): 10.5281/zenodo.18714577.
chain.nota: Selection values (according to the current visualization mode).
inv.p1: What does x^{1/5} do? It projects the desired value as x∈(0,1] using x^{1/5} in the Sᵢ family (smooth fractal contraction).
inv.p2: OFF (“pure r”): it searches for an r such that f(r)≈value without imposing x.
errors.16digits: Fill in the three fields with exactly 16 digits (excluding sign and dot).
errors.maxgtmin: Max must be greater than Min.
warn.swapMin: Observed is below Min. If it is correct, use it as the new minimum: swap “Observed” with “Min” and recalculate.
warn.swapMax: Observed exceeds Max. If it is correct, use it as the new maximum: swap “Observed” with “Max” and recalculate.

privacy.cuerpo:
{{app}} Visualize Coherences runs 100 % in your browser. We do not send your data to any server.

  • Data it handles: (a) inputs/values you enter, (b) saved patterns/templates and settings stored locally (IndexedDB/LocalStorage), (c) UI preferences (theme, language, filters, mode).
  • Local control: you can export or clear the local database whenever you want. Nothing is deleted outside your device.
  • Cookies: we do not use tracking or analytics cookies.
  • Security: your data stays on your machine. Avoid entering sensitive material on shared devices.
  • License and disclaimer: the software is provided “as is”, without warranties.

export.error.canvasMissing: Could not find the ellipse canvases.
coherence.title: Universal Coherence
help.autoderive.p1: When enabled, the app automatically recalculates S₁ and S₁₆ for viewing/copying, keeping the chain “closed” and coherent.
help.autoderive.li1: Useful if you’re exploring patterns and want the closure to be automatic.
help.autoderive.li2: If you want to lock S₁ or S₁₆ manually, turn this switch off.
help.projection.p1: Projection transforms the Sᵢ chain into a visual representation (bars and ellipses) to inspect structure, symmetries, sign, and magnitudes.
help.projection.li1: Bars: quick summary of sign and relative magnitude.
help.projection.li2: Ellipses: geometric projection by family using inversion (r) and, if applicable, x.
help.projection.li3: Apply filters and selection from the viewer to show only some Sᵢ.
coherence.text: May this work serve as gratitude and as a commitment: to think, feel, and act in the same direction, without "ornaments"; to keep creating with ethics, with joy, and with respect for everything that connects us.
